title: ArtifactType
description: Owl artifact type source body. Lives under `.owl/artifacts/<id>/artifact.yaml`. Defines the agent-facing template, required sections, and front matter schema for a reusable artifact type. The runtime validator (`Owl::Artifacts::Internal::ArtifactTypeValidator`) enforces a subset of this contract; the schema is the authoritative documented shape.

id.description: Artifact type identifier, lowercase snake_case; matches the registry key in `.owl/artifacts.yaml`.

kind.description: Artifact kind. Current values: `markdown`. Future kinds may declare different validators.

description.description: Optional one-paragraph description of the artifact's purpose.

default_template.description: Relative path (from the artifact type source directory) to a Markdown template that gets copied into a task's artifact path on first write.

front_matter.description: Schema for the YAML front matter block at the top of every artifact instance. Subset of JSON Schema validated by `Owl::Validation::Internal::FrontMatterValidator`.

validation.description: Body-level validation rules applied by `owl artifact validate`.

required_sections.description: Literal H1/H2 headings that must appear in the artifact body. Per constitution 5.16 these are always written in English.

required_patterns.description: Patterns that must match somewhere in the artifact body. Each entry is either a non-empty regex string, or a mapping `{ pattern, type?, level?, description? }` consumed by the runtime PatternsChecker — `type` is `regex` (default) or `substring`, `level` is `error` (default) or `warning`, and `description` is the violation message surfaced when the pattern is absent.

forbid_empty_sections.description: Opt-in semantic rule. When true, every heading must have non-whitespace body content (blank lines and HTML comments are ignored) before the next heading or EOF; otherwise an `empty_section` violation is raised.

forbid_placeholders.description: Opt-in semantic rule. `true` uses the default markers (TODO, TBD, FIXME, XXX, <...>); an array overrides them. Markers are matched as case-sensitive substrings outside fenced code blocks and raise `placeholder_text` violations.

require_scenarios.description: Opt-in semantic rule. When true, every `### Requirement` heading must be followed by at least one `#### Scenario` heading before the next level-3 heading or EOF; otherwise a `requirement_without_scenario` violation is raised.

require_when_then.description: Opt-in semantic rule. When true, every `#### Scenario` block must contain a WHEN line and a THEN line (leading bullets and bold tolerated); otherwise a `scenario_missing_clause` violation is raised.

agent_hints.description: Optional free-form hints surfaced to agents authoring this artifact (e.g. `summary_line_limit`, `tone`).
